response: Passengers landed at RAAF Base Pearce and moved to the Bullsbrook Centre for National Resilience for quarantine.
monitoring: Australian CDC described 42 days of quarantine from disembarkation, with at least the first 3 weeks at Bullsbrook.
caseStatus: No passenger had tested positive at the time of the Australian CDC update.
publicRisk: Not separately quantified in the cited update; response is focused on returning passengers.

response: PHAC reported one presumptive positive traveller and their spouse in hospital isolation, with a third high-risk individual transferred for assessment.
monitoring: PHAC said authorities are monitoring and isolating exposed individuals where appropriate.
caseStatus: One presumptive positive case was clinically stable with mild symptoms at the time of the PHAC update.
publicRisk: PHAC said the risk to the general Canadian population remains very low.

peopleAffected: US passengers and contacts managed through public-health follow-up
response: CDC reported that repatriated US passengers were evaluated and monitored through specialized care pathways.
monitoring: CDC advised exposed travellers to follow public-health monitoring instructions and seek care if symptoms develop.
caseStatus: CDC said no cases had been detected in the United States in its cited situation summary.
publicRisk: CDC described risk to the American public as extremely low.

response: UKHSA said returning passengers were transferred to Arrowe Park Hospital for clinical assessment, testing, and isolation planning.
monitoring: UKHSA described an isolation pathway of up to 45 days for returning passengers.
caseStatus: This tracker does not infer additional UK case counts beyond UKHSA or WHO reporting.
publicRisk: UKHSA described risk to the wider UK population as very low.

peopleAffected: Passengers and crew from multiple countries, including EU/EEA countries
response: ECDC continues to publish daily updates and support Member States on case finding, contact tracing, testing, isolation, and monitoring.
monitoring: ECDC describes 42 days of follow-up after last potential exposure for identified contacts.
caseStatus: ECDC reported 12 total cases as of 17 May: 9 confirmed, 2 probable, 1 inconclusive, 0 suspected, and 3 deaths.
publicRisk: ECDC assessed risk to the general population as very low.
